code:
import random

class RandomizedSet:

    def __init__(self):
        """
        Initialize your data structure here.
        """
        self.list = []  # Stores the elements
        self.map = {}   # Maps element value to its index in self.list

    def insert(self, val: int) -> bool:
        """
        Inserts a value to the set. Returns true if the set did not already contain the specified element.
        """
        if val in self.map:
            return False
        
        self.map[val] = len(self.list)
        self.list.append(val)
        return True

    def remove(self, val: int) -> bool:
        """
        Removes a value from the set. Returns true if the set contained the specified element.
        """
        if val not in self.map:
            return False
        
        # Get the index of the element to remove
        idx_to_remove = self.map[val]
        
        # Get the last element in the list
        last_val = self.list[-1]
        
        # Move the last element to the position of the element to be removed
        self.list[idx_to_remove] = last_val
        
        # Update the map for the moved element
        self.map[last_val] = idx_to_remove
        
        # Remove the last element from the list (which is now the duplicate of the removed element)
        self.list.pop()
        
        # Remove the deleted element from the map
        del self.map[val]
        
        return True

    def getRandom(self) -> int:
        """
        Get a random element from the set.
        """
        if not self.list:
            # Handle the case of an empty set.
            # Depending on requirements, could raise an error or return a specific value.
            return None 
        
        return random.choice(self.list)
